subject: Physics - Newtonian Mechanics
question: A body of mass $m = 5\text{ kg}$ is acted upon by a net force of $F = 20\text{ N}$. What is its acceleration?
solution: From Newton's second law: $F = ma \Rightarrow a = \frac{F}{m} = \frac{20}{5} = 4\text{ m/s}^2$.

subject: Physics - Kinematics
question: A projectile is launched horizontally from a height of $h = 45\text{ m}$ with velocity $v_0 = 15\text{ m/s}$. How far from the base of the launch point does it land? (Take $g = 10\text{ m/s}^2$)
solution:
Step 1 — Time of flight:
$h = \frac{1}{2}gt^2 \Rightarrow t = \sqrt{\frac{2h}{g}} = \sqrt{\frac{90}{10}} = 3\text{ s}$
Step 2 — Horizontal distance:
$x = v_0 t = 15 \times 3 = 45\text{ m}$

subject: Physics - Energy & Work
question: A spring with constant $k = 200\text{ N/m}$ is compressed by $x = 0.1\text{ m}$. What is the elastic potential energy stored in the spring?

subject: Physics - Circular Motion
question: A car of mass $m = 1000\text{ kg}$ travels over a circular hill of radius $R = 50\text{ m}$ at speed $v = 20\text{ m/s}$. What is the normal force on the car at the top of the hill?
solution:
At the top of the hill, gravity and normal force provide the centripetal acceleration (pointing downward toward center):
$$mg - N = \frac{mv^2}{R}$$
$$N = mg - \frac{mv^2}{R} = m\left(g - \frac{v^2}{R}\right) = 1000\left(10 - \frac{400}{50}\right) = 1000(10 - 8) = 2000\text{ N}$$

subject: Physics - Momentum & Collisions
question:
Two blocks slide toward each other on a frictionless surface. Block A has mass $m_A = 3\text{ kg}$ and velocity $v_A = +4\text{ m/s}$. Block B has mass $m_B = 2\text{ kg}$ and velocity $v_B = -6\text{ m/s}$.
(a) Find the total momentum of the system.
(b) After a perfectly inelastic collision, find the final velocity of the combined mass.
(c) Calculate the kinetic energy lost in the collision.
solution:
(a) Total momentum:
$p = m_A v_A + m_B v_B = 3(4) + 2(-6) = 12 - 12 = 0\text{ kg·m/s}$

(b) Final velocity after perfectly inelastic collision:
By conservation of momentum:
$p_f = (m_A + m_B)v_f = 0 \Rightarrow v_f = 0\text{ m/s}$
The combined mass is at rest.

(c) Kinetic energy lost:
$KE_i = \frac{1}{2}(3)(4)^2 + \frac{1}{2}(2)(6)^2 = 24 + 36 = 60\text{ J}$
$KE_f = 0$
$\Delta KE = 60\text{ J}$ (all converted to heat/deformation)
